# Motorcycle Parts Sales Analysis

## Overview

This project analyzes wholesale sales of motorcycle parts to identify the product lines, warehouses, and periods that generated the highest net revenue.

Using SQL for data transformation and Python for data visualization, the analysis focuses on understanding sales performance and uncovering patterns that can support business decision-making.

## Objectives

* Analyze net revenue generated by wholesale customers.
* Compare performance across product lines and warehouses.
* Identify monthly sales trends.
* Detect patterns that may reveal opportunities for operational or commercial improvement.

## Dataset

Motorcycle Parts Sales Dataset.

### Key Variables

| Variable      | Description                              |
|--------------|------------------------------------------|
| date         | Transaction date                         |
| product_line | Product category                         |
| warehouse    | Warehouse where the sale was processed   |
| client_type  | Customer segment                         |
| total        | Total sale amount                        |
| payment_fee  | Payment processing fee                   |

## Tools

* Python
* Pandas
* DuckDB
* Matplotlib
* Seaborn
* Jupyter Notebook

## Methodology

* Identified the business problem and relevant variables.
* Filtered records to include only wholesale customers.
* Calculated net revenue by subtracting payment fees from total sales.
* Aggregated results by product line, month, and warehouse using SQL.
* Visualized sales performance across different business dimensions.
* Analyzed patterns and trends to generate business insights.

## Key Findings

* Frame & Body generated the highest net revenue, followed by Suspension & Traction.
* Central was the best-performing warehouse, while West generated the lowest net revenue.
* Frame & Body and Suspension & Traction consistently contributed to revenue across warehouses.
* Net revenue remained relatively stable in June and July before increasing significantly in August.
* The growth observed in August was primarily driven by the Central and North warehouses.

## Conclusion

The analysis indicates that Frame & Body and Suspension & Traction were the main drivers of net revenue during the period analyzed. Additionally, the Central and North warehouses accounted for the strongest overall performance.

A noticeable increase in net revenue was observed in August, suggesting changes in sales activity that may warrant further investigation. In contrast, the lower performance of the West warehouse represents an opportunity for deeper operational or commercial analysis.

Given the descriptive nature of the study, the findings should be considered exploratory and may be complemented with additional metrics such as sales volume, profit margins, and profitability for a more comprehensive evaluation.
